vat_gst.name: USt (Umsatzsteuer / VAT)
vat_gst.registration_threshold_note: Below €22K, you can opt for Kleinunternehmerregelung (small business exemption) and skip VAT. Above €22K, VAT registration is mandatory. You can voluntarily register earlier to reclaim input VAT.
vat_gst.digital_products: Digital products sold to German/EU consumers are subject to 19% VAT. Use the EU OSS (One Stop Shop) to handle cross-border EU sales from a single registration.

digital_product_tax: Digital products (software, templates, SaaS, ebooks) are standard-rated at 19% USt. For EU cross-border B2C sales, use OSS to report VAT in a single return. B2B sales within the EU use the reverse charge mechanism. Non-EU sales are generally VAT-exempt.

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Income tax at personal rates (14-45%). No Gewerbesteuer if activity qualifies as 'freiberuflich' (software development usually qualifies).
entity_comparison.best_for: Solo software developers and consultants; simplest setup; no trade tax

entity_comparison.liability: Limited to company assets (minimum €1 share capital, but must retain 25% of profits until reaching €25K)
entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Körperschaftsteuer 15% + Solidaritätszuschlag 5.5% of that + Gewerbesteuer ~14-17% (varies by municipality). Effective rate ~30%.
entity_comparison.best_for: Low startup capital; stepping stone to GmbH; liability protection on a budget

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Same as UG. Effective rate ~30%. More credibility with enterprise clients.
entity_comparison.best_for: Established businesses; revenue above €100K/year; seeking enterprise contracts

isp_note: Deutsche Glasfaser expanding; DSL still common in rural areas
